posts.title: Top 5 Modern Pokémon TCG Sets for Long-Term Hold (2026 Edition)
posts.excerpt: Looking to grow your collection's value? Discover the top 5 modern Pokémon TCG sets from the Scarlet & Violet and Mega Evolution eras that are primed for long-term growth.
posts.body:
# The Smart Collector's Guide: Top 5 Modern Sets to Hold

In the early months of 2026, the Pokémon TCG market has seen a massive shift. With the launch of the **Mega Evolution series** and the continued dominance of nostalgia-driven sets, identifying which "modern" products to tuck away for the next five years is more critical than ever. 

If you are looking to build a "Blue Chip" Pokémon portfolio, these are the five sets you should be hunting for right now at **LI Collectors Co.**

---

### 1. Scarlet & Violet: 151 (The Nostalgia King)
Even three years after its initial release, **151** remains the gold standard for modern investing. By bringing back the original Kanto 151 in stunning Illustration Rares, this set captured the "Sword & Shield: Evolving Skies" level of demand. 

* **Why hold?** It’s a specialty set with no Booster Boxes, meaning ETBs and Booster Bundles are the primary way to hold sealed weight. As long as Charizard, Blastoise, and Venusaur are relevant, this set will appreciate.

### 2. Mega Evolution: Ascended Heroes (The New Heavyweight)
Released in January 2026, **Ascended Heroes** is officially the largest English set in history. It reintroduced Mega Evolutions to the TCG in a massive way, featuring the now-iconic **Mega Gengar ex** and **Mega Dragonite ex** Special Illustration Rares.

* **Why hold?** The sheer size of the set makes "Master Setting" nearly impossible through pulls alone, driving high demand for sealed booster boxes as the print run dries up later this year.

### 3. Scarlet & Violet: Prismatic Evolutions (The Eevee Factor)
You can never go wrong with Eeveelutions. **Prismatic Evolutions** (late 2024) features the most sought-after Umbreon and Sylveon cards since the Sun & Moon era. 

* **Why hold?** Eeveelution-focused sets have a historical track record of 100-200% growth once they go out of print. The Umbreon ex SIR is already a modern grail.

### 4. Mega Evolution: Perfect Order (The Sleeper Hit)
While everyone was chasing the 300-card sets, **Perfect Order** (March 2026) took a "quality over quantity" approach. With a smaller card list and incredible art for fan-favorites like Clefairy and Meowth, this set is the "sleeper" pick of the year.

* **Why hold?** Smaller sets are easier to complete, leading to a higher volume of "Master Set" collectors who will pay a premium for sealed product once it's no longer on store shelves.

### 5. Sword & Shield: Crown Zenith (The Modern Classic)
The final set of the Sword & Shield era is still widely considered the best "ripping experience" in a decade. With the Galarian Gallery featuring some of the best art ever printed on cardboard, **Crown Zenith** has maintained incredible price stability.

* **Why hold?** It is the last of its kind before the move to the "ex" era. Sealed ETBs are becoming increasingly scarce on Long Island and beyond, making this a "must-have" for any serious long-term vault.

---

### Final Thoughts for Investors
When holding modern sets, **condition is everything.** Whether you're looking for that PSA 10 contender or want to keep your booster boxes mint, storage is key. 

At **LI Collectors Co.**, we specialize in sourcing these high-potential sets. Check out our current inventory to see if we have these "Long-Term Hold" winners in stock today!

posts.title: PSA Grading: A Beginner's Guide to Submitting Your Pokémon Cards
posts.excerpt: Thinking about getting your cards graded but not sure where to start? We break down the PSA submission process step by step — from pre-screening to receiving your slab.
posts.body:
# PSA Grading: A Beginner's Guide

If you're sitting on a collection of raw Pokémon cards and wondering whether they're worth grading, you're not alone. PSA (Professional Sports Authenticator) is the gold standard in the collectibles hobby, and a PSA 10 label can multiply the value of a card many times over.

Here's everything you need to know before you submit your first card.

## What Is PSA Grading?

PSA grades cards on a scale of 1–10 based on four criteria:

- **Centering** — Is the printed image centered within the card borders?
- **Corners** — Are they sharp and undamaged?
- **Edges** — Any nicks, chips, or roughness?
- **Surface** — Scratches, print lines, staining, or creases?

A **PSA 10 (Gem Mint)** is the holy grail — near-perfect on all four dimensions.

## Step 1: Pre-Screen Your Cards

Before you spend money on submission fees, be honest about your card's condition. Hold it under direct light and rotate it slowly. Look for:

- White edge wear
- Surface scratches (especially on holographic cards)
- Off-center printing
- Any creases, even hairline ones

At Long Island Collectors Co., we offer free pre-screening for local customers. We'll give you an honest grade estimate before you commit to submission.

## Step 2: Choose Your Service Tier

PSA offers several service tiers based on your timeline and declared value:

| Tier | Declared Value | Turnaround |
|---|---|---|
| Economy | Up to $499 | 45–120 days |
| Regular | Up to $499 | 20–30 days |
| Express | Up to $2,499 | 10 business days |
| Super Express | Up to $4,999 | 5 business days |
| Walk-Through | Any | 3 business days |

For most beginners, **Economy** is the right starting point. The wait is longer, but the per-card fee is lowest.

## Step 3: Package and Ship

Proper packaging is critical. A card damaged in transit will not be graded at its true condition.

1. Place each card in a **card saver** (not a hard shell — PSA prefers flexible card savers)
2. Wrap card savers in bubble wrap
3. Ship in a rigid box with plenty of padding
4. Use a shipping service with **tracking and insurance** (USPS Priority Mail with declared value works well)

## Step 4: Wait and Track

Once PSA receives your submission, you can track it through their online portal. Status updates include: Received → Research & ID → Grading → Assembly → QC → Shipping.

Turnaround times fluctuate. During peak hobby seasons, even Express orders can run long.

## Step 5: Receive Your Slabs

When your cards return, they'll be sealed in PSA's signature sonically welded cases with a certification number. You can verify any PSA cert at **psacard.com/cert**.

## Is It Worth It?

For most vintage Pokémon cards in excellent raw condition: **yes**. A Base Set Charizard that grades PSA 9 can sell for exponentially more than its raw counterpart. Even modern rare full-arts in PSA 10 carry a meaningful premium.

For bulk commons and uncommons: probably not — fees outweigh the upside.

---

*Ready to get your cards graded? [Contact us](mailto:info@licollectorsco.com) — we handle submissions for local Long Island collectors and guide you through the entire process.*
